notes: Maximum run length of consecutive identical responses per respondent. The default cutoff, half the item count, is the 'greater than half the scale length' starting point of Meade & Craig (2012) applied to the full item set -- a heuristic (not a calibrated false-positive rate), lenient for multi-scale instruments, and user-adjustable; inspect the score distribution.
Intra-individual response variability (within-person SD across items); low values flag low-effort responding.
Even-odd consistency across within-scale item halves; reverse-keyed items are reverse-scored first.
Personal reliability (Jackson 1976) and its resampled variant (Goldammer et al. 2024, about 25 resamples); reverse-keyed items are reverse-scored first.
Psychometric synonyms: within-person correlation across strongly positively correlated item pairs; default critical r = 0.60.

Psychometric antonyms: within-person correlation across strongly negatively correlated item pairs; supply critical_r as a positive magnitude (default 0.60), which keeps pairs with inter-item r below -0.60.
Mahalanobis distance of each response vector from the sample centroid; chi-square cutoff at p = 0.001.
Person-total correlation (point-biserial form) of each respondent's responses with the whole-sample item means.

Normed polytomous Guttman-error index via PerFit Gnormed.poly; nonparametric, no IRT model; reverse-keyed items are reverse-scored first. Default cutoff is the PerFit Monte-Carlo null at a 0.05 nominal level.
Polytomous person-scalability Ht via mokken coefH on the transposed scale; nonparametric; reverse-keyed items are reverse-scored first.

Maximum absolute lag autocorrelation of the raw response vector (Gottfried et al. 2022); high values flag repetitive or periodic patterns. Computed on raw responses (no reverse-keying). Default cutoff is the empirical upper percentile, following the paper's relative-ranking recommendation. Standalone; not yet run by cier_screen.

First-order Markov response predictability Laz.R (Biemann et al. 2025): transition probabilities weighted by their counts; high values flag lazy or repetitive patterns. Default cutoff is the empirical upper percentile (the convention Biemann et al.'s own studies use, flagging the top 5%); the paper-faithful sample-specific Kneedle elbow is selectable via kneedle = TRUE on the wrapper. Standalone; not yet run by cier_screen.

Total survey completion time in seconds (Ward & Meade 2023; Huang et al. 2012); low values flag speeding. Default cutoff is the empirical lower percentile at fpr 0.05; a median-relative override (Leiner 2019; Greszki et al. 2015) is available on the wrapper. Standalone; not yet run by cier_screen.

Count of pages answered faster than 2 s per item (Bowling et al. 2023); any rapid page flags. A fraction-of-pages override (frac) and a literal count cutoff are available on the wrapper. Standalone; not yet run by cier_screen.

Count of failed attention checks (instructed-response, bogus, and infrequency items) among those answered (Meade & Craig 2012; Goldammer et al. 2024); any failure flags. Standalone; not yet run by cier_screen.
